ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: Ordinary Shadcn documentation composes TanStack Table into a Data Table with consumer-authored columns, sorting, filtering, pagination, visibility, and selection.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora owns a typed semantic-table API with controlled or uncontrolled sorting and native radio selection, labelled narrow-screen scrolling, and an optional live selection summary that never exposes TanStack types.

visualSignature.summary: The grid uses a literal Canvas table, Ink rules, strong caption and header hierarchy, shared Violet focus and sort context, restrained Green selected state, bounded corners, and a stable summary rail.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Consumers can render domain-appropriate selected-row context in a polite live status rail while retaining source-row typing.

api.enableWhen: Provide renderSelectionSummary, normally with selectionMode single.

disabledBehavior.behavior: Native table sorting and optional radio selection continue unchanged.
disabledBehavior.events: The selection-summary renderer is not invoked.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No output element or polite live region is exposed.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Automated native table, caption, scoped header, radio naming, focusable overflow, keyboard, narrow RTL layout, forced-colors, reduced-motion, and axe evidence is current across the component browser matrix; Risk Class 3 manual records remain incomplete.

interactionEvidence.summary: Native sorting, controlled and uncontrolled selection, empty state, summary updates, and complete disabled enhancement removal are covered for the semantic-table tracer.

parityEvidence.summary: Deterministic generation derives every package implementation byte from normalized canonical source and matches native and Shadcn payloads to the same consumer transform; the recorded exact-tarball Next.js and Vite package/source matrix passes.

blockers.summary: Interactive grid mode, editing, virtualization, server operations, responsive alternatives, permissions, and the full Risk Class 3 evidence matrix remain open.

maturityAssessment.rationale: Deterministic delivery parity is verified, but the remaining interaction, lifecycle, browser, accessibility, scale, security, or release evidence recorded for this item is still insufficient for promotion.

ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: Ordinary Shadcn has no first-party nested query-builder component; consumers typically compose form controls and own schema, reordering, validation, serialization, and summaries.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora provides a schema-bound nested query tree with deterministic serialization, non-drag reordering, recovery-focused validation, native form/reset behavior, and an optional localizable canonical summary.

visualSignature.summary: Literal Canvas fieldsets, Ink rules, strong legends, Violet nested-group context, Green validation/summary state, shared focus geometry, bounded corners, logical narrow layout, and preference fallbacks form a precise workbench hierarchy.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Consumers may expose a domain-localized human explanation of the complete nested query without changing the editable query model.

api.enableWhen: Set showSummary true and optionally provide renderSummary.

disabledBehavior.behavior: Nested editing, validation, serialization, form submission, and reordering continue unchanged.
disabledBehavior.events: renderSummary is not invoked and no summary-specific state update occurs.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No output element or additional summary text is exposed.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Automated nested semantics, native keyboard order, validation, form, narrow, RTL, forced-colors, reduced-motion, and axe evidence is current; Risk Class 3 manual records remain incomplete.

interactionEvidence.summary: Controlled/uncontrolled state, add/remove, nested groups, non-drag movement, validation, serialization, form reset, and exact summary isolation have focused automated evidence.

parityEvidence.summary: Deterministic generation derives every package implementation byte from normalized canonical source and matches native and Shadcn payloads to the same consumer transform; the recorded exact-tarball Next.js and Vite package/source matrix passes.

blockers.summary: Required desktop/mobile screen-reader, speech, switch, touch, zoom, and localization sessions are not recorded.

maturityAssessment.rationale: Deterministic delivery parity is verified, but the remaining interaction, lifecycle, browser, accessibility, scale, security, or release evidence recorded for this item is still insufficient for promotion.

ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: Ordinary Shadcn has no first-party filter-builder system; consumers compose fields, badges, dialogs, and URL state without a shared serialization or mobile contract.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora combines a concise native filter editor with deterministic Unicode-safe URL state, saved-filter application, a decision-safe active summary, and a focus-restoring mobile dialog, all independently selectable.

visualSignature.summary: Canvas fieldsets, Ink boundaries, strong filter legends, Violet context, Green active-summary state, shared focus, restrained corners, native modal structure, and logical responsive stacking repeat the Mergora workbench language.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Active summary, saved filters, URL synchronization, and mobile drawer are four separate switches with exact removal contracts.

api.enableWhen: Enable only the summary, saved-filter, URL, and mobile-dialog capabilities the workflow needs.

disabledBehavior.ui: Each false value independently removes its summary, selector, or drawer UI; urlAdapter=false adds no UI.
disabledBehavior.behavior: The plain inline filter editor remains fully operable without persistence or overlay behavior.
disabledBehavior.events: Disabled summary renderers, saved-filter applies, URL reads/writes, and drawer open/close paths cannot fire.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: Disabled enhancements expose no output, saved selector, dialog/trigger, live text, or extra relationships.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Automated native fieldset, modal dismissal/focus return, keyboard, narrow, RTL, forced-colors, reduced-motion, and axe evidence is current; Risk Class 3 manual records remain incomplete.

interactionEvidence.summary: Filter mutation, Unicode URL round-trip, saved application, form reset, mobile-dialog focus, and exact independent enhancement removal have focused automated evidence.

parityEvidence.summary: Deterministic generation derives every package implementation byte from normalized canonical source and matches native and Shadcn payloads to the same consumer transform; the recorded exact-tarball Next.js and Vite package/source matrix passes.

blockers.summary: Required desktop/mobile screen-reader, speech, switch, touch, zoom, and nested-overlay sessions are not recorded.

maturityAssessment.rationale: Deterministic delivery parity is verified, but the remaining interaction, lifecycle, browser, accessibility, scale, security, or release evidence recorded for this item is still insufficient for promotion.

ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: Ordinary Shadcn has no first-party sortable-list component; common compositions add a drag library but leave non-drag movement, cancellation, announcements, undo, and virtualization to consumers.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora treats drag as additive to visible move controls and a cancellable keyboard pickup model, with optional destination selection, localized announcements, undo, and bounded virtualization.

visualSignature.summary: Literal Canvas list rows, Ink structure, strong item/position hierarchy, Green committed context, Violet grabbed/focus state, target-sized handles, restrained corners, and immediate reduced-motion fallbacks provide a consistent spatial identity.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Destination controls, announcements, undo, and bounded virtualization can be selected independently while visible Move Up/Down remains the non-drag baseline.

api.enableWhen: Enable each assistance or scale feature independently for the collection.

disabledBehavior.ui: Destination selects, live status, Undo, and virtual spacers/window controls are each absent when disabled.
disabledBehavior.behavior: Pointer drag, cancellable keyboard pickup, and visible Move Up/Down remain available over the full list.
disabledBehavior.events: No destination, announcement, undo, or window callbacks/state paths occur for disabled features.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No destination labels/options, live region, Undo control, virtual spacers, or duplicate position output is exposed.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Automated ordered-list semantics, keyboard pickup/drop/cancel, non-drag actions, focus recovery, narrow, RTL, forced-colors, reduced-motion, and axe evidence is current; manual Risk Class 3 evidence remains incomplete.

interactionEvidence.summary: Pure movement, button/destination movement, keyboard cancellation, native pointer drop and Escape cancellation without mutation, undo, controlled/uncontrolled ownership, reset, and exact enhancement isolation have focused automated evidence.

parityEvidence.summary: Deterministic generation derives every package implementation byte from normalized canonical source and matches native and Shadcn payloads to the same consumer transform; the recorded exact-tarball Next.js and Vite package/source matrix passes.

blockers.summary: Required desktop/mobile screen-reader, speech, switch, touch, zoom, pointer-cancellation, and scale sessions are not recorded.

maturityAssessment.rationale: Deterministic delivery parity is verified, but the remaining interaction, lifecycle, browser, accessibility, scale, security, or release evidence recorded for this item is still insufficient for promotion.

ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: Ordinary Shadcn has no first-party ARIA treegrid; a Data Table composition does not supply hierarchical row semantics, direction-aware APG navigation, editing recovery, or virtual focus policy.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora owns a one-tab-stop hierarchical treegrid with controlled expansion/selection/active row, direction-aware two-dimensional navigation, optional recoverable editing, preserved form state, bounded virtualization, and an optional hierarchy summary.

visualSignature.summary: Literal Canvas cells, Ink rules, sticky strong headers, Green selection/status, Violet active hierarchy seams, shared focus geometry, restrained corners, logical indentation, and visible narrow overflow repeat the workbench language.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Hierarchy summary, change announcements, editing, and bounded virtualization are independently selected around the same APG navigation core.

api.enableWhen: Enable only the contextual, editing, announcement, and scale capabilities required by the grid.

disabledBehavior.ui: Summary, live status, editor/save/error UI, and virtual spacers/window controls are independently absent.
disabledBehavior.behavior: Hierarchical expansion, selection, and two-dimensional navigation remain available over all visible rows.
disabledBehavior.events: Disabled summary, announcement, edit/persistence, and virtual-window paths cannot fire.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No summary relationship, live region, edit stops/states, or virtual spacer output is exposed.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Automated treegrid role/state, APG keyboard, selection, focus recovery, editing, preferred coarse-pointer targets, narrow overflow, RTL, forced-colors, reduced-motion, and axe evidence is current; manual APG interoperability and Risk Class 3 records remain incomplete.

interactionEvidence.summary: Flattening, expansion, controlled/uncontrolled selection, direction-aware grid movement, edit cancellation/recovery, form reset, virtualization bounds, and exact enhancement isolation have focused automated evidence.

parityEvidence.summary: Deterministic generation derives every package implementation byte from normalized canonical source and matches native and Shadcn payloads to the same consumer transform; the recorded exact-tarball Next.js and Vite package/source matrix passes.

blockers.summary: Complete APG desktop/mobile screen-reader, speech, switch, touch, zoom, editing, and large-data sessions are not recorded.

maturityAssessment.rationale: Deterministic delivery parity is verified, but the remaining interaction, lifecycle, browser, accessibility, scale, security, or release evidence recorded for this item is still insufficient for promotion.

ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: Ordinary Shadcn has no first-party Kanban system; common card-and-drag compositions leave keyboard cancellation, non-drag movement, WIP policy, mobile parity, persistence recovery, and virtualization to consumers.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora makes pointer drag additive to explicit move controls and cancellable direction-aware keyboard movement, with independently selected WIP context, mobile list parity, announcements, undo, server rollback/retry, and bounded virtualization.

visualSignature.summary: Literal Canvas columns/cards, Ink boundaries, strong headings, Green capacity seams, Violet Beta/grabbed/focus state, target-sized handles, restrained corners, and logical mobile/RTL structure repeat the Mergora workbench language.

optionalEnhancements.summary: WIP status, mobile list presentation, announcements, undo, server persistence, and bounded virtualization are independently selectable around the explicit non-drag baseline.

api.enableWhen: Enable each contextual, mobile, feedback, recovery, persistence, or scale capability independently.

disabledBehavior.ui: Capacity text, view controls, live status, Undo, pending/error/retry UI, and virtual window output are independently absent.
disabledBehavior.behavior: Pointer drag, cancellable keyboard movement, visible move buttons, destination controls, and WIP rejection remain usable.
disabledBehavior.events: No disabled view, announcement, undo, server, rollback/retry, or window callback path can fire.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No WIP text, presentation group, live region, Undo/retry control, busy state, or virtual spacer output is exposed.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Automated ordered-list semantics, keyboard pickup/drop/cancel, explicit alternatives, WIP rejection, mobile presentation, narrow, RTL, forced-colors, reduced-motion, and axe evidence is current; required physical mobile and Risk Class 3 manual records remain incomplete.

interactionEvidence.summary: Pure cross-column movement, native pointer drop and Escape cancellation without mutation, non-drag controls, direction-aware keyboard cancellation, WIP limits, mobile list, server rollback/retry, undo, virtualization, and exact enhancement isolation have focused automated evidence.

parityEvidence.summary: Deterministic generation derives every package implementation byte from normalized canonical source and matches native and Shadcn payloads to the same consumer transform; the recorded exact-tarball Next.js and Vite package/source matrix passes.

blockers.summary: Kanban must remain Beta until physical iOS VoiceOver and Android TalkBack move workflows pass and are reviewed.
Required screen-reader, speech, switch, touch, zoom, server-recovery, and large-board sessions are not recorded.

maturityAssessment.rationale: Deterministic delivery parity is verified, but the remaining interaction, lifecycle, browser, accessibility, scale, security, or release evidence recorded for this item is still insufficient for promotion.
